q: From which part of Zeus's body was the goddess Athena born?
exp: Athena sprang fully grown and armoured from Zeus's head.

q: Who was Zeus's father, whom he overthrew?
exp: Cronus (Saturn in Rome) devoured his children; Zeus defeated him.

q: Who ferried souls across the river Styx?
exp: A coin was paid to Charon to cross the river of the dead.

q: Which monster had heads of a lion, goat and serpent and breathed fire?
exp: The Chimera was slain by Bellerophon riding Pegasus.

q: Which Norse god lost a hand in the mouth of the wolf Fenrir?
exp: Tyr sacrificed his hand so the gods could bind the wolf Fenrir.

q: Which giant wolf, son of Loki, would kill Odin at Ragnarök?
exp: Fenrir was chained by the gods until Ragnarök.

q: What is the giant serpent that encircles the world in Norse myth?
exp: Jörmungandr, the World Serpent, is Thor's great enemy.

q: Which Greek hero killed Medusa by looking at her reflection in a shield?
exp: Perseus avoided being turned to stone by viewing only her reflection.

q: What were the three goddesses of fate in Greek mythology?
exp: The Fates spun, measured and cut the thread of life.

q: How many Muses were there, goddesses of the arts?
exp: The nine Muses inspired poetry, music and dance.

q: Which Roman two-faced god gave his name to the month of January?
exp: Janus looked to the past and the future — hence 'January'.

q: What is the name of the realm of humans in Norse mythology?
exp: Midgard was the world of men, at the centre of Yggdrasil.

q: Which goddess ruled the world of the dead in Norse mythology?
exp: Hel, daughter of Loki, ruled over Helheim, the world of the dead.

q: Which two ravens brought Odin news of the world?
exp: Huginn ('thought') and Muninn ('memory') flew the world and reported to Odin.

q: What is the name of Odin's spear, which never missed?
